task_completion.description: Record acute mountain sickness diagnosis for Roberto Carlos
task_completion.visual_evidence: GNU Health interface showing the patient 'Roberto Carlos' with a recorded diagnosis or condition of 'Acute mountain sickness' (or an ICD-10 code starting with T70).

task_completion.description: Record clinical evaluation findings for hypoxia
task_completion.visual_evidence: Clinical evaluation or vital signs form in GNU Health showing Oxygen Saturation (SpO2) recorded at 88% or lower.

task_completion.description: Record clinical evaluation findings for compensatory tachycardia
task_completion.visual_evidence: Clinical evaluation or vital signs form in GNU Health showing Heart Rate (HR) recorded at 100 bpm or higher.
Prescription or medication order screen in GNU Health showing 'Acetazolamide' or 'Dexamethasone' prescribed for the patient.

task_completion.description: Order a blood gas or metabolic laboratory panel
task_completion.visual_evidence: Laboratory test request screen in GNU Health showing an order for a blood gas or metabolic panel.

task_completion.description: Schedule an acute follow-up appointment within 1 to 3 days
task_completion.visual_evidence: Appointment scheduling screen in GNU Health showing a follow-up appointment booked for Roberto Carlos with a date strictly 1 to 3 days from the current system date.

integrity.description: Agent used the GNU Health GUI to complete the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show navigation and data entry within the GNU Health graphical user interface, rather than terminal commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual application fields
integrity.visual_evidence: Input fields, dropdown menus, and save buttons in GNU Health are actively used and populated with the required medical data.

integrity.description: Agent did not use direct database manipulation to bypass the UI
integrity.visual_evidence: Absence of terminal windows running SQL commands (e.g., psql) or python scripts modifying the GNU Health database directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Patient Name: Roberto Carlos. Diagnosis: Acute mountain sickness (ICD-10 code starting with T70). Clinical thresholds required: SpO2 <= 88% and Heart Rate >= 100 bpm. Medication required: Acetazolamide or Dexamethasone. Lab order: At least 1 (blood gas or metabolic panel). Follow-up appointment: strictly within 1 to 3 days from the current date.
